---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-23"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(pROC)

```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# Load data
diabetes <- read.csv("diabetic_data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Check structure
str(diabetes)
dim(diabetes)
summary(diabetes)

# Check outcome distribution
table(diabetes$readmitted)

# Convert ? to NA
diabetes[diabetes == "?"] <- NA

# Create binary outcome
diabetes$readmit30 <- ifelse(diabetes$readmitted == "<30", 1, 0)

# Convert outcome to factor
diabetes$readmit30 <- factor(diabetes$readmit30, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))

# Check new outcome
table(diabetes$readmit30)

# Remove ID columns
diabetes <- diabetes %>%
  select(-encounter_id, -patient_nbr, -readmitted)

# Check missing values
missing_summary <- sapply(diabetes, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
sort(missing_summary, decreasing = TRUE)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



```{r}
model_data <- diabetes %>%
  select(readmit30, race, gender, age, admission_type_id,
         discharge_disposition_id, admission_source_id,
         time_in_hospital, num_lab_procedures, num_procedures,
         num_medications, number_outpatient, number_emergency,
         number_inpatient, number_diagnoses, max_glu_serum,
         A1Cresult, insulin, change, diabetesMed)

str(model_data)
summary(model_data)
```
```{r}
model_data_clean <- na.omit(model_data)
dim(model_data_clean)
```
